title: Handling Events
description: Respond to user actions like button presses and text input.

slides.title: Event Handlers 🖱️
slides.content:
# Listening to User Actions

In React Native, components like `Button`, `TouchableOpacity`, and `TextInput` accept props that are functions, called when certain events occur.

## Common Events

* `onPress` – for buttons and touchables.
* `onChangeText` – for text input (gives the new text).
* `onSubmitEditing` – when the user submits the text input (e.g., presses return).
* `onFocus` / `onBlur` – when an input gains/loses focus.

These event handlers are just functions you define.

slides.title: Handling Text Input
slides.content: A controlled text input that updates state as the user types:

quizData.question: Which prop do you use on TextInput to get notified when the text changes?
quizData.explanation: `onChangeText` is called with the new text string each time the user types.

slides.title: Inline vs Separate Functions 🔧

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Notice we used `onChangeText={setEmail}` directly. This works because `setEmail` expects exactly one argument – the new value – and `onChangeText` passes that. It's a handy shorthand.
